title: rosa canina var. spuria
intro: Rosa canina var. spuria, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. spuria include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to help with digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Rosa canina var. spuria can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support overall health and well-being.
